meta.subtitle: Roadmap
meta.currentState: Manager Agent local MVP is ready: scored routing, approval gates, persistence, run-log dashboard visibility, service inspection endpoints, and routing/approval evals are implemented. Live OpenAI smoke test remains blocked by API quota/billing.
meta.nextFocus: Unblock API billing/quota and run a live Manager Agent smoke test; if still blocked, start the Social Agent MVP on the Manager foundations.

stats.label: Overall
stats.detail: Real progress based on completed project work: dashboard scaffold exists, Manager Agent local MVP now has routing, approval gates, persistence, run-log visibility, and eval coverage. Live OpenAI validation, scheduler, specialist agents, and integrations are still pending.

stats.label: Agents
stats.detail: Ten agent roles are mapped as requirements, not implemented services.

stats.label: Ready
stats.detail: The roadmap dashboard, Manager Agent local MVP, and Manager routing/approval eval suite are ready for review.

stats.label: Decision Items
stats.detail: These decisions should be frozen before core development begins. API billing/quota is now tracked because live OpenAI calls reached the API but were rejected by current quota.

agents.name: Manager
agents.purpose: Central dispatcher for requests, routing, approvals, schedules, and operational visibility.
todos: Run live OpenAI smoke test after billing/quota is unblocked
Add run-log dashboard filters for agent, status, and approval state
Promote file-store schema to PostgreSQL when production begins
risks: Live OpenAI validation is blocked until API billing/quota is fixed
Do not connect publishing or client-write tools until approvals are tested in live mode
agents.statusAction: Local Manager MVP is ready. Next blocker: enable API billing/quota and run the live OpenAI smoke test.

agents.name: Develop
agents.purpose: Builds web/app work, writes tests, opens pull requests, and prepares deployments.
agents.compact: Code, review, test, PR, deploy handoff.
risks: Production changes without review
Client repo credentials must stay isolated
agents.statusAction: Wait until core task and approval system is frozen, then design the GitHub workflow.

agents.name: Design
agents.purpose: Produces UI directions, brand systems, campaign visuals, and handoff-ready creative briefs.
risks: Off-brand outputs
Unlicensed assets entering client work
agents.statusAction: Freeze the client brand kit schema after the Manager Agent requirements are stable.

agents.name: Video
agents.purpose: Plans short-form and campaign video scripts, storyboards, captions, and edit briefs.
risks: Wrong claims in ad scripts
Missing permissions for client media
agents.statusAction: Keep planned until creative asset and approval structures are defined.

agents.name: Google
agents.purpose: Handles SEO, Google Ads planning, keyword strategy, landing-page checks, and performance reports.
risks: Ad spend changes need strict permission
Analytics data can be noisy or delayed
agents.statusAction: Start with read-only reporting before any ad budget action is allowed.

agents.name: Social
agents.purpose: Creates platform-specific calendars, posts, hooks, campaigns, and approval-ready drafts.
risks: Publishing without approval
Tone mismatch across clients
agents.statusAction: Prepare as the first specialist after Manager because it gives fast agency value.

agents.name: Sales
agents.purpose: Researches leads, drafts outreach, prepares proposals, and keeps pipeline records current.
risks: Outbound messages need approval
Lead data must be sourced responsibly
agents.statusAction: Choose the first CRM and map required pipeline fields.

agents.name: Support
agents.purpose: Handles client follow-ups, weekly summaries, support requests, and account health signals.
risks: Client-facing messages need approval
Support issues can hide delivery risks
agents.statusAction: Define support lifecycle after Manager task states are connected.

agents.name: QA
agents.purpose: Reviews output quality, factual accuracy, brand fit, permissions, and publish readiness.
risks: Weak governance makes every other agent unsafe
Review rules can become too vague
agents.statusAction: Create the first cross-agent QA checklist before any publishing tools are connected.

agents.name: Accounts
agents.purpose: Tracks invoices, retainers, subscriptions, payment follow-ups, and project profitability notes.
risks: Financial messages require approval
Sensitive payment data must stay protected
agents.statusAction: Define accounting workflow after core CRM and client records are selected.

phases.title: Requirement Freeze
phases.summary: Turn this dashboard into the shared source for roadmap, requirements, tasks, and decisions. Manager Agent lifecycle, approval rules, local persistence shape, and routing/approval eval expectations are now frozen.
phases.todo: Keep new scope behind Decisions Board before specialist agents are built.
phases.statusAction: Next: keep decisions tight while moving into specialist MVP work.

phases.title: Independent Core
phases.summary: Build owned backend, database, scheduler, queue, and file/memory foundations. Manager Agent service endpoints, file-backed task/run persistence, and dashboard run mirroring are implemented.
phases.todo: Promote the local file schema to PostgreSQL when production storage begins.
phases.statusAction: Production persistence direction is PostgreSQL + pgvector after the local file-store schema is proven.

phases.title: Agent MVP
phases.summary: Ship Manager, Social Market, and Sales agents with tool calls and approval gates. Manager local MVP is ready; Social and Sales remain pending.
phases.todo: Run live Manager smoke test when quota is unblocked, then start Social Agent MVP.
phases.statusAction: Manager is locally ready; next specialist candidate is Social.

phases.title: Delivery Agents
phases.summary: Add Development, Design, Video, and Google Market workflows after the control layer works.
phases.todo: Define safe tool permissions per specialist.
phases.statusAction: Start after MVP agents are reliable.

phases.title: Agency OS
phases.summary: Unify dashboards, reports, costs, schedules, evals, and role-based access.
phases.statusAction: Start after core workflows are proven.

stack.name: React + Tailwind
stack.next: Use this dashboard as the requirements surface.

stack.name: OpenAI Responses API
stack.next: Manager Agent live mode is coded, but live output remains blocked until API quota or billing is enabled.

stack.name: PostgreSQL + pgvector
stack.next: Selected production persistence direction after the local file-backed task/run schema is validated.

stack.name: Redis or open-source queue
stack.next: Add for scheduled and background agent jobs.

stack.name: Docker
stack.next: Use for local database and service orchestration.

stack.name: GitHub + Codex
stack.next: Use the work-round flow: update code, test, update roadmap, commit, push.

workRound.title: Codex Work Round
triggerModes: Direct request: complete the requested roadmap or implementation task, even if it changes scope.
Next focus request: find the active nextFocus item, complete the highest-priority work it implies, then update roadmap state.
steps: Read roadmap.json and current repository state.
Identify next focus by nextFocus=true, priority, statusAction, and Decisions Board state.
Implement the smallest useful work package.
Run build and browser checks until clean.
Update agent progress, phase progress, status, decisions, and the nextFocus item.
Commit and push to GitHub for deployment.
workRound.lastCompleted: Added Manager routing/approval eval suite, upgraded routing to scored matching, exposed task/run inspection endpoints, and marked the local Manager MVP ready.

approvalMatrix.approver: Business owner or assigned account lead
Business owner or client-approved content lead

approvalMatrix.action: Create drafts, reports, briefs, or internal tasks

decisions.label: CRM choice
decisions.detail: Pending. Choose the first CRM target before Sales Agent implementation. Recommended low-dependency path: start with a simple internal pipeline table, then connect HubSpot, Zoho, or Pipedrive only if needed.

decisions.label: MVP agent order
decisions.detail: Decided. Build order starts with Agency Manager, then Social Market and Sales. This gives routing, approvals, content planning, and lead workflow before deeper integrations.

decisions.label: Approval roles
decisions.detail: Decided for MVP. Client email, publishing, ad budget changes, deployment, and production data edits require human approval. Drafts, reports, briefs, and internal task creation may proceed with logs.

decisions.label: Client file schema
decisions.detail: Pending. Define the required client knowledge folders: brand, offers, services, assets, reports, meetings, approvals, and credentials references.

decisions.label: Backend language
decisions.detail: Decided. Use Node first because this machine already has Node and the roadmap dashboard is Vite/React. The Manager Agent can call the OpenAI Responses API directly now; Python/FastAPI can be added later only if a specialist workflow needs it.

decisions.label: Weekly reports
decisions.detail: Pending. Decide the first recurring report format: client summary, active tasks, wins, blockers, next-week plan, and metrics snapshot.

decisions.label: API billing quota
decisions.detail: Pending. Live OpenAI smoke test reached the API but returned a current quota/billing error. Enable API billing or adjust usage limits before live agent runs can complete.

decisions.label: Production persistence
decisions.detail: Decided. Use PostgreSQL + pgvector as the production persistence backend after the local file-backed task and run-log schema is validated. Keep the JSON file store as the local prototype and dashboard mirror.
